title: hieracium ctenodon subsp. triglochinum
intro: Hieracium ctenodon subsp. triglochinum is a type of flowering plant, also known as hawkweed, a member of the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe and protect wounds. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the leaves and roots of Hieracium ctenodon subsp. triglochinum are typically used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential applications.
